mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 1-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering institutional risk-off rebalancing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly supports near-term corporate revenue guidance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production remains above the 100 index baseline, subsequent monthly gains signal effective capacity utilization and pricing power, leading to margin expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are flat or negative in > 50% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 100 and rises, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for sector weight shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A cross below the 2.5% annualized inflation threshold removes the primary constraint on Federal Reserve policy flexibility, compressing real yields and expanding equity risk premiums to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity multiples fail to expand when inflation crosses below 2.5%, or 60-day forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from the pre-crossing baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential monthly increases in retail sales directly feed into quarterly earnings revisions for consumer-facing equities, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns in rising sales months are not significantly higher than falling sales months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly payroll growth exceeds 180,000 while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, the market prices in a prolonged restrictive policy stance to combat wage-driven inflation, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX term structure changes.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion characterized by two consecutive quarters of decelerating real GDP growth, rising housing starts lead to inventory accumulation and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of decelerating GDP quarters, or the signal's predictive power disappears after adjusting for dividend yield changes.
